{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月十八|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 18일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 18''' is the 18th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Butsumetsu|ja|仏滅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu|1=Butsumetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10886789}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Datong (disambiguation)|en|Datong (disambiguation)|WD=Q295082|lt=Daidō}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|806|en|806|WD=Q30021|lt=806}} {{ill|June 8|en|June 8|WD=Q2621|lt=June 8}}) – Prince Andon ascended the throne and became the 51st Emperor, later known as {{ill|Emperor Heizei|en|Emperor Heizei|WD=Q353558|lt=Emperor Heizei}}. * (The first year of Datong – June 8, 806 in the Julian calendar – with the enthronement of Emperor Heijō, the era name was changed from {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku}} to {{ill|Datong (disambiguation)|en|Datong (disambiguation)|WD=Q295082|lt=Daidō}}.) * {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1862|en|1862|WD=Q7698|lt=1862}} {{ill|June 15|en|June 15|WD=Q2613|lt=June 15}}) – The Sosho Observatory was moved outside the Hitotsubashi Gate and renamed the Western Book Bureau. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869}} {{ill|June 27|en|June 27|WD=Q2664|lt=June 27}}) – Takeaki Enomoto and others opened the last stronghold, {{ill|Goryōkaku|en|Goryōkaku|WD=Q1196357|lt=Goryokaku}}, and surrendered to the new government army. (This marked the end of the Boshin War and the unification of Japan under the Restoration government.) * (Meiji 2, June 27, 1869 in the Gregorian calendar – alternative account of the above event.)
== Births == * {{ill|Hōen|en|Hōen|WD=Q1193929|lt=Hoen}} 5th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1139|en|1139|WD=Q19608|lt=1139}} {{ill|June 16|en|June 16|WD=Q2653|lt=June 16}}) – {{ill|Emperor Konoe|en|Emperor Konoe|WD=Q373997|lt=Konoe Tennō}}, the 76th Emperor of Japan (died {{ill|1155|en|1155|WD=Q19650|lt=1155}}). * {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 13rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1842|en|1842|WD=Q7625|lt=1842}} {{ill|June 26|en|June 26|WD=Q2661|lt=June 26}}) – {{ill|Matsumura Junzō|ja|松村淳蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumura Junzō|1=Matsumura Junzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531438}}, a military personnel (died {{ill|1919|en|1919|WD=Q2157|lt=1919}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Shōkyō|en|Shōkyō|WD=Q1069086|lt=Shōkyō}} 2nd year / {{ill|Genkō (1321–1324)|en|Genkō (1321–1324)|WD=Q509889|lt=Genkō}} 3rd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1333|en|1333|WD=Q6004|lt=1333}} {{ill|June 30|en|June 30|WD=Q2657|lt=June 30}}) – {{ill|Hōjō Moritoki|en|Hōjō Moritoki|WD=Q1083395|lt=Hōjō Morotoki}}, the 16th Shikken of the Kamakura shogunate (born {{ill|1295|en|1295|WD=Q5560|lt=1295}}). * {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 15th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1587|en|1587|WD=Q6744|lt=1587}} {{ill|June 23|en|June 23|WD=Q2668|lt=June 23}}) – {{ill|Ōmura Sumitada|en|Ōmura Sumitada|WD=Q2728800|lt=Ōmura Sumitada}}, a Christian daimyō who dispatched the Tenshō mission to Europe (born {{ill|1533|en|1533|WD=Q6443|lt=1533}}). * {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|WD=Q1145262|lt=Hōei}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1705|en|1705|WD=Q6830|lt=1705}} {{ill|July 8|en|July 8|WD=Q2692|lt=July 8}}) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|en|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|WD=Q313745|lt=Tokugawa Tsunayoshi}}, the 3rd lord of the Kishū Domain (born {{ill|1665|en|1665|WD=Q7582|lt=1665}}).

== Events == * {{ill|Datong (disambiguation)|en|Datong (disambiguation)|WD=Q295082|lt=Daidō}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|806|en|806|WD=Q30021|lt=806}} {{ill|June 8|en|June 8|WD=Q2621|lt=June 8}}) – Prince Andon ascended the throne and became the 51st Emperor, later known as {{ill|Emperor Heizei|en|Emperor Heizei|WD=Q353558|lt=Emperor Heizei}}. * (The first year of Datong – June 8, 806 in the Julian calendar – with the enthronement of Emperor Heijō, the era name was changed from {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku}} to {{ill|Datong (disambiguation)|en|Datong (disambiguation)|WD=Q295082|lt=Daidō}}.) * {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1862|en|1862|WD=Q7698|lt=1862}} {{ill|June 15|en|June 15|WD=Q2613|lt=June 15}}) – The Sosho Observatory was moved outside the Hitotsubashi Gate and renamed the Western Book Bureau. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869}} {{ill|June 27|en|June 27|WD=Q2664|lt=June 27}}) – Takeaki Enomoto and others opened the last stronghold, {{ill|Goryōkaku|en|Goryōkaku|WD=Q1196357|lt=Goryokaku}}, and surrendered to the new government army. (This marked the end of the Boshin War and the unification of Japan under the Restoration government.) * (Meiji 2, June 27, 1869 in the Gregorian calendar – alternative account of the above event.)
== Births == * {{ill|Hōen|en|Hōen|WD=Q1193929|lt=Hoen}} 5th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1139|en|1139|WD=Q19608|lt=1139}} {{ill|June 16|en|June 16|WD=Q2653|lt=June 16}}) – {{ill|Emperor Konoe|en|Emperor Konoe|WD=Q373997|lt=Konoe Tennō}}, the 76th Emperor of Japan (died {{ill|1155|en|1155|WD=Q19650|lt=1155}}). * {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 13rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1842|en|1842|WD=Q7625|lt=1842}} {{ill|June 26|en|June 26|WD=Q2661|lt=June 26}}) – {{ill|Matsumura Junzō|ja|松村淳蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumura Junzō|1=Matsumura Junzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531438}}, a military personnel (died {{ill|1919|en|1919|WD=Q2157|lt=1919}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Shōkyō|en|Shōkyō|WD=Q1069086|lt=Shōkyō}} 2nd year / {{ill|Genkō (1321–1324)|en|Genkō (1321–1324)|WD=Q509889|lt=Genkō}} 3rd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1333|en|1333|WD=Q6004|lt=1333}} {{ill|June 30|en|June 30|WD=Q2657|lt=June 30}}) – {{ill|Hōjō Moritoki|en|Hōjō Moritoki|WD=Q1083395|lt=Hōjō Morotoki}}, the 16th Shikken of the Kamakura shogunate (born {{ill|1295|en|1295|WD=Q5560|lt=1295}}). * {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 15th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1587|en|1587|WD=Q6744|lt=1587}} {{ill|June 23|en|June 23|WD=Q2668|lt=June 23}}) – {{ill|Ōmura Sumitada|en|Ōmura Sumitada|WD=Q2728800|lt=Ōmura Sumitada}}, a Christian daimyō who dispatched the Tenshō mission to Europe (born {{ill|1533|en|1533|WD=Q6443|lt=1533}}). * {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|WD=Q1145262|lt=Hōei}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1705|en|1705|WD=Q6830|lt=1705}} {{ill|July 8|en|July 8|WD=Q2692|lt=July 8}}) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|en|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|WD=Q313745|lt=Tokugawa Tsunayoshi}}, the 3rd lord of the Kishū Domain (born {{ill|1665|en|1665|WD=Q7582|lt=1665}}).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|17|5|19|4|18|6|5|0518|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 18 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月18日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 18일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 18 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月十八]][[zh:五月十八]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 18 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月18日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 18일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 18 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月十八]][[zh:五月十八]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 18 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月18日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 18일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 18 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月十八]][[zh:五月十八]]
{{Not to be confused with|May 18|qq=May 18}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?-->
{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月十八|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 18일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 18''' is the 18th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Butsumetsu|ja|仏滅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumetsu|1=Butsumetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10886789}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Datong (disambiguation)|en|Datong (disambiguation)|WD=Q295082|lt=Daidō|1=Datong (disambiguation)}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|806|en|806|WD=Q30021|lt=806|1=806}} {{ill|June 8|en|June 8|WD=Q2621|lt=June 8|1=June 8}}) – Prince Andon ascended the throne and became the 51st Emperor, later known as {{ill|Emperor Heizei|en|Emperor Heizei|WD=Q353558|lt=Emperor Heizei|1=Emperor Heizei}}. * (The first year of Datong – June 8, 806 in the Julian calendar – with the enthronement of Emperor Heijō, the era name was changed from {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} to {{ill|Datong (disambiguation)|en|Datong (disambiguation)|WD=Q295082|lt=Daidō|1=Datong (disambiguation)}}.) * {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū|1=Bunkyū}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1862|en|1862|WD=Q7698|lt=1862|1=1862}} {{ill|June 15|en|June 15|WD=Q2613|lt=June 15|1=June 15}}) – The Sosho Observatory was moved outside the Hitotsubashi Gate and renamed the Western Book Bureau. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}} {{ill|June 27|en|June 27|WD=Q2664|lt=June 27|1=June 27}}) – Takeaki Enomoto and others opened the last stronghold, {{ill|Goryōkaku|en|Goryōkaku|WD=Q1196357|lt=Goryokaku|1=Goryōkaku}}, and surrendered to the new government army. (This marked the end of the Boshin War and the unification of Japan under the Restoration government.) * (Meiji 2, June 27, 1869 in the Gregorian calendar – alternative account of the above event.)
== Births == * {{ill|Hōen|en|Hōen|WD=Q1193929|lt=Hoen|1=Hōen}} 5th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1139|en|1139|WD=Q19608|lt=1139|1=1139}} {{ill|June 16|en|June 16|WD=Q2653|lt=June 16|1=June 16}}) – {{ill|Emperor Konoe|en|Emperor Konoe|WD=Q373997|lt=Konoe Tennō|1=Emperor Konoe}}, the 76th Emperor of Japan (died {{ill|1155|en|1155|WD=Q19650|lt=1155|1=1155}}). * {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} 13rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1842|en|1842|WD=Q7625|lt=1842|1=1842}} {{ill|June 26|en|June 26|WD=Q2661|lt=June 26|1=June 26}}) – {{ill|Matsumura Junzō|ja|松村淳蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumura Junzō|1=Matsumura Junzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531438}}, a military personnel (died {{ill|1919|en|1919|WD=Q2157|lt=1919|1=1919}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Shōkyō|en|Shōkyō|WD=Q1069086|lt=Shōkyō|1=Shōkyō}} 2nd year / {{ill|Genkō (1321–1324)|en|Genkō (1321–1324)|WD=Q509889|lt=Genkō|1=Genkō (1321–1324)}} 3rd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1333|en|1333|WD=Q6004|lt=1333|1=1333}} {{ill|June 30|en|June 30|WD=Q2657|lt=June 30|1=June 30}}) – {{ill|Hōjō Moritoki|en|Hōjō Moritoki|WD=Q1083395|lt=Hōjō Morotoki|1=Hōjō Moritoki}}, the 16th Shikken of the Kamakura shogunate (born {{ill|1295|en|1295|WD=Q5560|lt=1295|1=1295}}). * {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 15th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1587|en|1587|WD=Q6744|lt=1587|1=1587}} {{ill|June 23|en|June 23|WD=Q2668|lt=June 23|1=June 23}}) – {{ill|Ōmura Sumitada|en|Ōmura Sumitada|WD=Q2728800|lt=Ōmura Sumitada|1=Ōmura Sumitada}}, a Christian daimyō who dispatched the Tenshō mission to Europe (born {{ill|1533|en|1533|WD=Q6443|lt=1533|1=1533}}). * {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|WD=Q1145262|lt=Hōei|1=Hōei}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1705|en|1705|WD=Q6830|lt=1705|1=1705}} {{ill|July 8|en|July 8|WD=Q2692|lt=July 8|1=July 8}}) – {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|en|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|WD=Q313745|lt=Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|1=Tokugawa Tsunayoshi}}, the 3rd lord of the Kishū Domain (born {{ill|1665|en|1665|WD=Q7582|lt=1665|1=1665}}).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|17|5|19|4|18|6|5|0518|qq=}}
